mechanisms.label: Discount
mechanisms.definition: A reduction in council tax liability calculated after liability and valuation have been established, usually as a percentage of charge.

mechanisms.label: Disregard
mechanisms.definition: A rule that removes a person from the count of relevant adults used to determine discount or exemption outcomes.

mechanisms.label: Exemption
mechanisms.definition: A rule that removes or extinguishes council tax liability for a dwelling or class of occupation.

mechanisms.label: Premium
mechanisms.definition: An increase applied to the standard charge for certain long-term empty or second-home situations.

mechanisms.label: Reduction
mechanisms.definition: A reduction scheme or band adjustment that changes the charge payable, including disabled band reduction and CTS.

mechanisms.label: Support
mechanisms.definition: Means-tested or scheme-based reduction such as local council tax support.

mechanisms.label: Sanction
mechanisms.definition: A formal enforcement outcome applied following fraud or incorrect claims, including cautions, penalties and prosecution.

mechanisms.label: Civil penalty
mechanisms.definition: A fixed financial penalty applied for negligent or incorrect information without requiring prosecution.

mechanisms.label: Payment flexibility
mechanisms.definition: A discretionary or operational option that changes payment dates or spreads instalments without changing liability.

mechanisms.label: Special payment arrangement
mechanisms.definition: A catch-up payment arrangement for missed council tax instalments. Before summons it may be available as a self-serve online option; after summons it becomes a council-managed assessed arrangement.

legal_basis_types.label: Local discretionary
legal_basis_types.definition: A local authority discretionary scheme or policy choice.

legal_basis_types.label: Statutory
legal_basis_types.definition: A rule or entitlement based directly on legislation.

discount_categories.label: Disregard
discount_categories.definition: A person-count treatment that can indirectly create a discount or exemption outcome.

discount_categories.label: Person based
discount_categories.definition: A discount or disregard determined by the status, circumstances or attributes of one or more people.

discount_categories.label: Property based
discount_categories.definition: A discount or reduction determined by the physical use, occupation or relationship of a dwelling.

discount_categories.label: Student related
discount_categories.definition: A student-specific disregard or exemption class driven by education status.

exemption_categories.label: Occupied
exemption_categories.definition: An exemption category applying where the dwelling is occupied by a qualifying class of resident.

exemption_categories.label: Unoccupied
exemption_categories.definition: An exemption category applying where the dwelling is unoccupied and the last or intended occupier meets a qualifying condition.

exemption_classes.label: B
exemption_classes.definition: Empty dwelling owned by a charity and exempt for the limited statutory period.

exemption_classes.label: D
exemption_classes.definition: Empty dwelling where the liable person is detained in prison or other qualifying detention.

exemption_classes.label: E
exemption_classes.definition: Empty dwelling where the liable person has moved permanently to a hospital or care home.

exemption_classes.label: F
exemption_classes.definition: Empty dwelling because the liable person has died and probate or letters of administration conditions apply.

exemption_classes.label: G
exemption_classes.definition: Dwelling occupation prohibited by law.

exemption_classes.label: H
exemption_classes.definition: Empty dwelling awaiting occupation by a minister of religion.

exemption_classes.label: I
exemption_classes.definition: Empty dwelling where the liable person is receiving care elsewhere.

exemption_classes.label: J
exemption_classes.definition: Empty dwelling where the liable person has moved elsewhere to provide care.

exemption_classes.label: K
exemption_classes.definition: Empty dwelling left by a student.

exemption_classes.label: L
exemption_classes.definition: Dwelling repossessed by a mortgagee.

exemption_classes.label: M
exemption_classes.definition: Student hall of residence.

exemption_classes.label: N
exemption_classes.definition: Dwelling occupied only by students.

exemption_classes.label: O
exemption_classes.definition: Armed forces accommodation.

exemption_classes.label: P
exemption_classes.definition: Visiting forces accommodation.

exemption_classes.label: Q
exemption_classes.definition: Dwelling left empty by a bankrupt trustee.

exemption_classes.label: R
exemption_classes.definition: Unoccupied caravan pitch or boat mooring.

exemption_classes.label: S
exemption_classes.definition: Occupied only by persons under 18.

exemption_classes.label: T
exemption_classes.definition: Unoccupied annexe that may not be let separately due to planning condition.

exemption_classes.label: U
exemption_classes.definition: Occupied only by people who are severely mentally impaired.

exemption_classes.label: V
exemption_classes.definition: Occupied by at least one foreign diplomat or specified international organisation person.

exemption_classes.label: W
exemption_classes.definition: Occupied annexe used by a dependent relative.

occupancy_statuses.label: Sole or main residence
occupancy_statuses.definition: The dwelling is occupied as a person's sole or main residence.

occupancy_statuses.label: Second home
occupancy_statuses.definition: The dwelling is furnished but is not anyone's sole or main residence.

occupancy_statuses.label: Empty long-term
occupancy_statuses.definition: The dwelling has been unoccupied for a long enough period to trigger empty-property rules.

property_statuses.label: Domestic dwelling
property_statuses.definition: A dwelling entered in the council tax list.

property_statuses.label: Holiday let
property_statuses.definition: Accommodation let or intended to be let for short stays; council tax or business rates treatment depends on statutory tests.

property_statuses.label: Self-catering accommodation
property_statuses.definition: Self-catering accommodation that may move between council tax and business rates depending on VOA rules.

property_statuses.label: House in multiple occupation
property_statuses.definition: A house in multiple occupation for which owner-liability rules may apply.

property_statuses.label: Annexe
property_statuses.definition: A subsidiary dwelling linked to a main residence.

person_roles.label: Liable person
person_roles.definition: A person who is legally responsible for paying council tax for a dwelling.

person_roles.label: Resident
person_roles.definition: A person who occupies a dwelling as their sole or main residence.

person_roles.label: Owner
person_roles.definition: The person or body with the legal ownership interest that makes them liable where owner liability applies.

person_roles.label: Student
person_roles.definition: A person who meets the statutory definition of a full-time student for council tax purposes.

person_roles.label: Care leaver
person_roles.definition: A person who qualifies under the authority's care leaver reduction scheme.

liability_bases.label: Resident hierarchy
liability_bases.definition: Liability is established by the statutory hierarchy of resident status and interest in the dwelling.

liability_bases.label: Owner liability
liability_bases.definition: Liability rests with the owner where owner-liability classes apply or there are no liable residents.

liability_bases.label: Property based charge
liability_bases.definition: The base charge is attached to the dwelling rather than to income or benefit status.

liability_bases.label: Valuation band
liability_bases.definition: The dwelling charge is derived from the statutory valuation band.

evidence_types.label: Interview under caution record
evidence_types.definition: Record of the interview under caution including any admission.

evidence_types.label: Signed caution certificate
evidence_types.definition: Signed record confirming acceptance of a caution.

evidence_types.label: Overpayment calculation
evidence_types.definition: Calculation showing the value and period of the overpayment.

evidence_types.label: Penalty agreement
evidence_types.definition: Signed agreement for a penalty as an alternative to prosecution, including cooling-off rights.

decision_outcomes.label: Apply
decision_outcomes.definition: The mechanism or rule outcome should be applied.

decision_outcomes.label: Reject
decision_outcomes.definition: The mechanism or rule outcome should not be applied.

decision_outcomes.label: Pending evidence
decision_outcomes.definition: A decision cannot be completed until required evidence is supplied.

decision_outcomes.label: Review required
decision_outcomes.definition: A human decision or review is required before the outcome can be finalised.

decision_outcomes.label: Blocked for publication
decision_outcomes.definition: The content or result must not be published until blockers are resolved.

decision_outcomes.label: Sanction applied
decision_outcomes.definition: A sanction or civil penalty outcome has been selected for the case.

decision_outcomes.label: Support path
decision_outcomes.definition: A recovery outcome focused on support, affordability and signposting rather than immediate escalation.

decision_outcomes.label: Enhanced support path
decision_outcomes.definition: A tailored support outcome used where vulnerability or hardship requires modified handling.

decision_outcomes.label: Enhanced enforcement path
decision_outcomes.definition: A more assertive recovery outcome used where the resident is assessed as unwilling rather than unable to pay.

decision_outcomes.label: Special payment arrangement
decision_outcomes.definition: A negotiated replacement instalment plan used as the primary pre-enforcement intervention where policy conditions are met.

decision_outcomes.label: Post-summons arrangement
decision_outcomes.definition: A council-managed arrangement outcome selected at summons or liability order stage after financial assessment.

issue_types.label: Validation gap
issue_types.definition: A content or contract area that cannot yet be fully validated from trusted sources.

issue_types.label: Sign-off gap
issue_types.definition: A review or approval requirement that is still outstanding.

issue_types.label: Publication blocker
issue_types.definition: An issue that prevents safe public release.

source_types.label: compiled
source_types.definition: A curated composite source assembled from multiple primary or secondary sources.

source_types.label: gcc_cabinet_report
source_types.definition: A Gloucester City Council cabinet or committee report.

source_types.label: user_provided_policy_extract
source_types.definition: A policy extract provided directly by the user in conversation for schema integration work.

themes.label: annexe-discount
themes.definition: Discount for qualifying annexes associated with a main dwelling.

themes.label: apprentices-youth-trainees
themes.definition: Disregard for apprentices and specified youth trainees.

themes.label: care-leavers
themes.definition: Local discount for qualifying care leavers.

themes.label: carer-disregard
themes.definition: Disregard for qualifying carers providing substantial care.

themes.label: disabled-band-reduction
themes.definition: Reduction for dwellings with qualifying disability-related features.

themes.label: foreign-spouses-dependants-of-students
themes.definition: Disregard for qualifying foreign student spouses or dependants.

themes.label: job-related-second-home
themes.definition: Discount for qualifying job-related second homes.

themes.label: major-repair
themes.definition: Historic or legacy discount/guidance for major repair situations.

themes.label: school-college-leavers
themes.definition: Temporary disregard for certain 18 to 19 year old leavers and child-benefit-linked cases.

themes.label: severely-mentally-impaired
themes.definition: Household outcome driven by SMI disregard status.

themes.label: single-person-discount
themes.definition: Discount where only one counted adult is resident.

themes.label: fraud-and-sanctions
themes.definition: Fraud investigation outcomes, sanctions ladder decisions, civil penalties and prosecution escalation.

themes.label: Compassionate recovery
themes.definition: A recovery approach that considers vulnerability, hardship, dignity and ability to pay before escalation.

relationship_types.label: Derived from
relationship_types.definition: Relationship indicating that one artefact element is derived from another.

relationship_types.label: Governed by
relationship_types.definition: Relationship indicating that an element is controlled by a rule, policy or legal basis.

relationship_types.label: Cannot pay
relationship_types.definition: Resident is assessed as lacking the means to clear the debt on standard terms.

relationship_types.label: Temporary hardship
relationship_types.definition: Resident is facing a short-term financial shock affecting ability to pay.

relationship_types.label: Vulnerable
relationship_types.definition: Resident has indicators requiring modified, safeguarded or referred-back handling.

relationship_types.label: Engaging but struggling
relationship_types.definition: Resident is cooperating and seeking an affordable route to pay.

relationship_types.label: Non-engaging
relationship_types.definition: Resident is not responding or not providing required information.

relationship_types.label: Will not pay
relationship_types.definition: Resident appears able to pay but refuses meaningful engagement or repeatedly defaults without reasonable explanation.
